close_reason: Collected all credential errors into Vec<String>, final error shows all attempts
description:
src/lib.rs:214-220: 'last_err = Some(e)' overwrites on every iteration. If MI fails with 'role assignment missing' and developer creds also fail with 'azure-cli not logged in', the user sees only the developer-cred error. The MI failure — the actual production problem — is invisible.

Maintenance consequence: a class of issue reports of the form 'I get "azure-cli not logged in" in production' where the real root cause is MI misconfiguration. Each one will require asking the user to run with credential=managed-identity to see the real error.

Fix: on chained-credential failure, return a synthesized error that contains all source errors (e.g. 'all credential sources failed: MI: <err1>; developer: <err2>'). azure_core::Error supports with_context for this; alternatively wrap into a SecretError::Backend with a Display impl that lists all sources.
title: ChainedCredential discards intermediate errors; only final error visible
